message:
fix(core): credential broker scope enforcement, HLL rho edge case, search result ordering

Credential broker: request_lease now checks that the requested scope is
a subset of the credential's permitted_scopes before proceeding to
authorization. Previously, a credential could be used with any scope if
the connector-level authorization passed. Revoke now correctly
decrements the provider's active_leases counter and increments
total_revoked for all leases invalidated during the revocation. Tests
added for scope-exceeds-permission denial and provider counter updates.

HyperLogLog: extracted index_and_rho() helper from duplicated code in
insert() and insert_hash(). Fixed an edge case where hash values with
all-zero remaining bits (after extracting the bucket index) would
produce rho=1 instead of the correct value (65 minus precision). This
affected cardinality estimates when hash collisions concentrated in
low-index buckets. Test added for the zero-remaining-bits case.

Search indexing: search results now preserve the relevance-ranked order
from the scoring phase instead of re-sorting by recency after collecting
results. The previous sort-by-captured_at step was overriding the
carefully computed BM25+TF-IDF-based ranking, causing older but more
relevant documents to be demoted below newer but less relevant ones.
Test added verifying that a document with 3 keyword occurrences ranks
above a newer document with 1 occurrence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
